cot_content:
Okay, let's see. The question is about how to tell right and left earbuds apart without looking. The options are to tie a knot either at the connecting end or the speaker end of the left one.

Hmm, I think the idea is that when you're using earbuds, you have them in your ears, so you can't see them. So the knot should be in a place that you can feel when you're handling the earbuds. If the knot is at the connecting end (the part that plugs into the device), then when you're plugging or unplugging, you might feel it. But if the knot is at the speaker end (the part that goes in the ear), then when you're putting them in your ear, you'd feel the knot and know which is left.

Wait, but you can't feel the speaker end when it's in your ear. So maybe the knot needs to be on the part you can feel when you're handling the earbuds. So if you take them out of your ears, and you're holding the wire, the knot would be on the left one at the speaker end. But when you take them out, you can look at them. Wait, the question says "without looking," so the method should work when you can't see them.

So if the knot is on the connecting end (the part that connects to the device), when you're plugging them in, you can feel the knot and know which one is the left. But if it's on the speaker end, you'd feel the knot when you're putting them in your ear. But once they are in your ear, you can't see them, but you can feel the knot on the part that's in your ear. Wait, that might not work because the knot is inside the ear. So maybe the knot should be on the part that's on the outside, the connecting end. That way, when you're handling the earbuds (like plugging them in or taking them out), you can feel the knot and know which is left.

So the answer is probably A. Tie a small knot at the connecting end of the left one. That way, when you're handling the earbuds, you can feel the knot on the left one's connecting end and distinguish them without looking.
label: minority
